threat_model: Research source conflict mapping can accidentally become live retrieval, source contact, raw source-body retention, prompt-source instruction authority, answer synthesis, memory write, or publication if evidence and authority boundaries are not explicit. The mitigation is a citation-bound schema and validator that reject raw storage and deny all mutation authority in Foundation Mode.

required_checks.check: research source conflict map live search, source contact, connector, synthesis, publication, and terminal authority remain false
redaction check confirms question, claim, and summary evidence remain digest refs and raw source bodies remain null
IDOR check confirms tenant scope stays foundation-local-only and follow-up sensing cannot contact external sources
receipt integrity check confirms SearchDecision, SearchReceipt, evidence classification, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms conflict citation refs are drawn from the source set
proof coverage matrix includes the research source conflict map surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode map blocks live search, source contact, connector calls, answer synthesis, current-claim authority, memory writes, publication, terminal closure, and success claims.
Foundation Mode map requires digest refs, null raw source bodies, raw source-body retention denial, private payload redaction, and operator review.

mitigations: keep ResearchSourceConflictMap validation non-live and deterministic
require citation refs and digest refs instead of raw source bodies or connector payloads
require SearchDecision, SearchReceipt, UAO, and LifeMeaningJudgment refs
deny live search, source contact, connector calls, answer synthesis, and memory writes
deny publication, terminal closure, and success claims in Foundation Mode
